page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU capabilities, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core performance and PassMark CPU score, both ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions like AES and VAES, which align with its strong PassMark CPU encryption test results. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including read and write operations, fall within the average range.

The hardware profile presents specific resource tradeoffs. While the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without virtualization overhead, the 2.0 GB of memory per core limits its applicability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. Additionally, the lack of local NVMe storage requires all I/O operations to traverse the 32 Gbps network interface, which may impact latency-sensitive storage workloads.

This instance is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as multi-threaded decompression, web serving, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference. It achieves strong performance in prompt processing and text generation for 135M parameter models, and average performance for 7B parameter models. It is also appropriate for database operations and parallel processing tasks that fit within the 128.0 GB memory envelope.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz, the instance provides 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and cryptographic execution, while single-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable execution, though the 2.0 GB of memory per core presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads, including parallel processing, database operations, static web serving, and small-to-medium language model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 32 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 32 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and high-throughput network connectivity for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-optimized 1:2 resource ratio
32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and multi-threaded decompression performance
Suited for database operations, parallel processing, and medium-scale LLM inference
